The Upgraded FAQ (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 5
Speaker 1: You guys welcome to Themeplate 11, this is the upgraded FAQ and this really is a staple
in just about every launch campaign so feel free to use this as part of your minimum
viable funnel. The FAQ is traditionally used to counter objections but that's not all it can
do, it's actually capable of significantly more. When done right the upgraded FAQ
actually has a power to counter objections, position you against competitors, reinforce
the big ah-ha's that you instill during the prelaunch and create safety by pre-paving the
outcome while also helping them identify as a buyer all without breaking form.
It's pulling a lot of weight, it's really doing it's work, and I usually like to roll this email
out about 48 hours before the close, in other words it's that last email we will send it
during the sale sequence before getting in to the closing sequence which you'll learn in
module seven. The general format is this, you know you do want to lead in and frame
those questions or even skepticism as positive traits that are in line with it being a
buyer. Then the FAQ, this is once again a flexible themeplate don't try to make it too
rigid and try to shoehorn your copy and your words into this, but I usually like to
reference this model here as the eight types of questions that I will use.
Question one will be the biggest objection and transform it into a big opportunity so a
little bit of kind of like objection alchemy there if you will. Question two will take
another objection and use it to point to a specific and unique aspect of your product
that addresses it. Then question three will bust one of those major industry wide myths,
those sacred cows, just really reminding them of that so that's how you start re-
contextualizing and bolstering those sales arguments instead of diluting them. Four I like
to position against competitors and really get buy in from my prospect on my unique
way of doing things so this is very much a positioning thing, this is still trying to keep
them in the frame of solution aware now because they have already been like
symptomatically diagnosed, they've gotten a real problem, and now they're bridging
into solution aware but we want to make sure that ours is the only logical solution so
this where it's important to start positioning against your competitors.
Then we're gonna use question five sometimes as a springboard into your fuller USP or
unique selling proposition, you'll see how that plays out in just a bit. Question six, this is
pretty universal the time objection, you know will I have time to do this? Will I have
time to learn French? Will I have time to play guitar? Will I have time to create my
branding on my website? Will I have time, time, time? That's just kind of one of those
universal objections and this will be your chance to work that one in and I mean one
thing that's been consistent across this about everything I've done is people aren't afraid
of spending time they're afraid of wasting time so prove to them that they could get a
return on time, that every second, every minute they invest into your program, into
your training is going to have that pay off, that's what they need to hear here at this
point.
They don't need you to say it's only gonna be an hour a week or 20 minutes per week or
five hours a week or whatever it takes, they don't need that kind of data they just need
to know that what they do invest, whether it is 10 minutes, five hours, whatever it is is
going to have a payoff. The you could actually springboard into the offer while keeping
frame within the Q and A itself then use your risk reversal, your guarantees as well like
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

prominent in the closing sequence but since this email, this themeplate in particular is
just kind of like on the boarder of the close we do bring in some of that risk reversal and
guarantee stuff.
That is it and here is kind of like that's kind of the general format and the themeplate,
the organizing container for it, looks a little something like this. We will intro, describing
your reader but making it sound like your just really describing your best prospect or not
sound like you're simply describing your prospect but actually describing your best
prospect. If you've had calls with customers as soon as they've come in and asked them,
"What made you join? You know what objections did you overcome?" If you've gotten
those testimonials use that and go deeper into those conversations because you do
want to make this as accurate as possible to your real buyers and your best prospects.
Validate the reader, tell them you appreciate that, skepticism is a good thing, validate it,
meet it in their current context of where they're at. Instead of resisting and rejecting
that welcome it, skepticism is a good thing. Then why you appreciate that person
connected to the product you're selling, why that might make them a good fit, why that
might qualify them to be perfect for this program or product. Then tell them what
you're about to tell them, this is when you're just gonna kind of transition and jump into
your FAQ, then we're gonna go into the Q and A's, you could choose those eight
variations of it that we looked at above and repeat that for as many as you want to work
with, being tonal where appropriate.
You're just gonna organize those questions almost like a sales page so you're gonna
open as we show with more of those problem and objection related questions and
finishing with the closing ones like what happened exactly when they come into the
program, what they'll receive, if they need help ordering, and all those risk reducers. It
kind of does function likes sales page in that respect and then you'll have your sign off.
You could come back to add some urgency if you're closing in 48 hours, once again you
don't want to ramp it up if you still have more than 48 hours left, followed by your final
call to action.
That is this themeplate in a nut shell, I'm gonna run through an example that we actually
did for Courses That Convert. Questions people ask before saying yes to Courses That
Convert, you know we had a lot of data, we had a lot of information, so it was kind of
easy to pull this out but you could describe what your best customers have been asking
so that's really what we're aiming for here. The business owners who get the most out
of my programs are usually the ones who ask the most questions so making that in line
with the buyers journey questions aren't a bad thing.
The ones who refuse to let me off the hook, the ones who hold my feet to the fire and
dare to ask the hard-nosed questions personally I love it so we're inviting it, we're
showing we're not phased by that. At the end of the day I only want you in Courses That
Convert if you know without a doubt that it's the missing link between you and a fully
leveraged and profitable online business. Linking it to the outcome they want to
achieve, so without further ado here are the most common questions I've been fielding

Page 3 of 5
over the last few days. If you're still on the fence chances are at least one or two of
them has been wreaking havoc on your sleep patterns.
This is kind of specific too, during a launch you know when this new program is out and
it's starting to get into those 48 hours I actually have lost sleep so that's not super
untypical, sorry about that. Let's jump in, then we dive into the questions. You know
some of those bigger concerns like what if there are other courses on my topic? This
was one of the bigger objections or sacred cows about their ability to succeed, it's
awesome, that means there's a market, and an opportunity because where there's
competition there are also dissatisfied customers who didn't get their problems solved
the first time so the biggest objection to a biggest opportunity and how to do it. Your
job is to discover how current courses have let them down, note the failure points, and
create a course that rescues them from those issues and helps them achieve the results
they're looking for.
Second objection, what if I don't have a list? Sure it would be awesome to go into it with
an active list with your success certainly won't depend on it, there's a crazy myth that
you need to have a list of subscribers that trust you enough to tell you what they want
but in reality that's just one of many validation strategies, busting that myth and then
pointing to an element of your program that solves it. In module one you'll learn how to
gauge market interest and create a course that your market wants even if you don't
have an email list to send surveys to, yet. I call it the minimal viable validation method
and it's a perfect way to get up and running fast, no list needed. We just took the
second biggest objection that customers were experiencing and linked it to a valuable
and important part of the course.
How long do I need to be creating free content, et cetera, before I create my course? I
bet you've heard something along the lines of you need to give free, valuable content
for six, nine, 12 months before you've earned the right to create a course. Where does
this even come from? As long as there's a problem your target audience experiences
that your uniquely equipped to solve, it doesn't matter if you've been offering free
content for 10 years or 10 days. Yes being in the game longer may give you more
insights into what the market wants but these aspects can easily be overcome with the
strategies you will learn. In short you don't need permission, you need proper strategy
and implementation. We took another thing that was extremely disempowering to
them, this myth that's been holding them back, and we disarmed it while re-
contextualizing it with our offer. This is something we would have talked about in the
prelaunch or the beginning of the webinar which serves as a prelaunch but now in the
sales sequence we're re-contextualizing it within the realm of the offer.
I've seen other course building programs that also go deep into the marketing side. Does
Courses That Convert do it? This is all about positioning and in this case we could take
something that would be seen as negative such as not including too many modules on
the marketing and promotional side, instead turn that into a legitimate positive that
actually allows them to maintain 100% of focus on the task at hand and allowing them
to finish building an asset and this is huge, this is a game changer for many students
because one of the things that derails progress is trying to market it as you build it and
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

just wearing too many hats at the same time. This course really focused its USP on
creating that asset as quickly and as powerfully as possible and creating something that
is designed to be able to sell later because you've gone through all the right steps. That
is number four, linking it to the USP.
Conversion is a two sided process, yes marketing funnels and promotional strategies are
sexy but as I'm sure you've experienced it also gets overwhelming and leads you down
an endless rabbit hole of delay and distraction. I want to remind you that what you're
doing inside Courses That Convert is building a real profitable asset that can generate
income for the rest of your life, it's a process that requires laser focus and meticulous
step by step detail and a disciplined approach that back burns all tactics that don't serve
the immediate goal of building your course. I know this will turn off the shiny object
seekers but that's okay, my obligation isn't to them it's to you the driven, focused
entrepreneur who's made the big decision to build a valuable, long term asset with as
little distraction as possible.
Here you're just re-qualifying your prospect and affirming your way of doing things
versus your competitors. If you do have a slight case of funnel fever, don't we all, you'll
get access to a very special bonus course launch master plan. We do satisfy that itch
without overwhelming them with scratching, we don't let them derail their progress and
the true outcome of the course.
Then we have that universal time objection, you know will I really have time to build my
own online course? In short the busier you are the more you need a leveraged freedom
building, profit generating, online course in your business. It's always good to ask is
there a catch 22 here, if your audience is time starved does that mean they need what
you're selling even more? Yeah so that's something that is gonna be applicable across a
broad, broad range of topics and courses.
I get it, you're a super busy entrepreneur with a million things and maybe even a day job
on your plate. That's why Courses That Convert was built with one thing in mind,
implementation. We've laid out all the steps in crystal clear detail so you don't have to
worry about being your own project manager, instead you just have to block off a
couple hours a week, dive in, and follow the step by step processes that guide you
towards your first profitable online course. Once again, people aren't afraid of spending
time, they're afraid of not getting a return on that time. Just by encouraging them that
they get to plug themselves into this system and that everything they're working in it is
going to lead to that resolve that that is encouraging, that's what the audience needs to
hear for the time objection.
A lot of people would have just stopped, no you could just get started with like four
hours a week and you'll be fine. It's not the time they're concerned about, it's the return
on that time so I really invite you to explore how return on time would apply to you,
your course, and your audience. How many hours will I need to invest every week? This
is just a continuation of that getting a little more concrete, once you enroll you'll have
instant access. I'd say a minimum you should be ready to invest is two to three hours, a
small investment considering you're building an asset that could be worth more than

most people's homes. This is just kind of a nice little metaphor that ties it in to the value
of what they're really building here. Whenever you do mention how many hours they
are going to be spending it's great to contrast that, to anchor that against the value that
they're gonna receive from that investment.
Then we are getting into those more closing elements. Can you remind me of what I get
when I enroll today? Then we go through that walkthrough so this could be similar to
that Aladdin style we did in the launch sequence but yeah you don't have to go module
by module, you could say the full program and just really do that in a general way,
outline the bonuses, really kind of show what they're getting, so that operates kind of as
that mini-sales page. Then getting into the risk reversals so is it really risk free, give your
guarantee, really kind of take away all anxiety around that, and with your close if that
road leads you to Courses That Convert then I look forward to meeting you on the
inside.
That is the upgraded FAQ in a nutshell. What we're doing here is we are really framing
our questions both one as a positive thing that's in line with being a buyer. These are
legitimately questions that people ask before buying the program so we create that
identify with being a buyer and then we really walk them through a lot of the elements
that a sales page would starting with those more surface level objections, tying that
back to unique aspects of your product, making those objections opportunities, and
really just kind of walking them through that process, holding their hand, and just bring
them deeper and deeper towards that close.
That is it, this is one of those core emails I'll turn to over and over again across pretty
much every launch. I invite you to spend some time with it, don't get too hung up on the
order of the questions, just follow that general path that we outlined here, but there is
room for some flexibility here so don't get too hung up once again in trying to shoehorn
in and make it follow that exact format, just make sure that you're using kind of that
general orientation around it and that you're hitting the beats that we're really trying to
coach here. That's it, I will take a break and I'll meet you in Themeplate number 12.
